action.reasoning: Observation: No workspace files yet; need a full offline-capable pipeline and must submit an early eval. Analysis: 3D geometric models should outperform SMILES baselines on QM9; SchNet from PyG is available and simple to implement quickly. Decision: write a minimal SchNet training/prediction pipeline with cached preprocessing, train for a few epochs only, save best checkpoint, test predict.py locally, and submit eval immediately to validate the setup and measure runtime.
action.command: python train.py --epochs 4 --batch_size 128 --num_workers 4 && mkdir -p local_test && cp /data/val_3d.pt local_test/test_3d.pt && python predict.py --data_path local_test --checkpoint checkpoints/best.pt --output local_test/preds.json && python -c "import json; p=json.load(open('local_test/preds.json'))['predictions']; print(len(p), p[0])"
